vars._comment: Optional. Reusable prefixes; ${name} in any config value or a projects/*.md 'path:' expands to these, so a repo-root move is a one-line change.

frameworkRoot: (optional) ${code}/meta-os — defaults to resolving the instance's systems/ symlink
claudeHome: (optional) /Users/you/.claude — engine home whose session logs feed the usage widget; defaults to ~/.claude

memory._comment: Optional. Configurable memory topology. Omit this whole block and memory falls back to the default: the instance's own memory/{raw,wiki,output} as the sole canon root plus vaults/* as federated mounts — byte-for-byte the prior behaviour. 'roots' are canon (counted as the raw→wiki→output pipeline); each has a 'layout' of flat | tier/project | project/tier. 'federated' mounts are navigation context, never counted as canon. ${var} expands here exactly as in backlogs. Broken/dangling paths are skipped and reported (topology.skipped), never fatal.

auth._comment: OIDC — Google (now) or Tessera (later). enabled = UI login gate. enforce = API requires JWT (Google id_token). allowedEmails strongly recommended so random Google accounts cannot access your vault.
